notes: iter11 D-alt single-event force-final test. ChatGLM page loaded clean (no DevTools) at https://chatglm.cn/main/alltoolsdetail. User message '내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567 입니다' submitted successfully (visible as 访客_31fd04 bubble). After 60s wait, an empty 'ChatGLM' assistant header rendered but the bubble body remained COMPLETELY empty — no warning text, no think bubble, no tool_calls, no RRN decoding. DOM inspection confirmed zero text content in assistant response. Engine intercept clearly fired (response arrived and assistant header materialized), but D-alt single-event envelope was REJECTED by Vuex/renderer — actually WORSE than iter10 (#984 PARTIAL_THINK_ONLY at least rendered the think bubble; iter11 renders nothing at all). Eliminating intermediate think+tool_calls phases and presenting only status=finish text:finish from start did not trigger render — Vuex state machine appears to require the multi-phase progression (think→tool_calls→text) and silently drops single-shot finish-only envelopes. Strategy verdict: D-alt is a regression vs iter10. Need different approach (e.g., D-streamed: send think:running→think:finish→text:running→text:finish as separate SSE chunks to match real wrtn temporal progression).
